title: Auto-Save on Checkbox Selection and Soft Delete on Deselection
story: As a DataTools Pro user, I want dropdown checkbox selections to save automatically and deselections to trigger a soft delete, so that I can manage my selections without an extra save button step.
description:
User feedback indicated that the extra "Save Objects" button step after selecting items in the dropdown was unclear. The improvement removes the manual save step by:

1. **Checkbox check**: Automatically saves the selection immediately
2. **Checkbox uncheck**: Triggers a soft delete (using the same delete confirmation modal as the delete icon)

This eliminates the intermediate save button, making the dropdown selection workflow more intuitive and reducing the number of steps.
acceptance_criteria: Checking a checkbox in the dropdown immediately saves the selection
Unchecking a checkbox triggers a soft delete with a confirmation modal
The 'Save Objects' button is removed from the dropdown
Soft delete uses the same confirmation modal as the delete icon action
Previously saved selections are reflected when reopening the dropdown
ux_changes: Removed the manual 'Save Objects' button from the dropdown. Checkbox selections now auto-save on check and trigger a soft delete confirmation modal on uncheck.
middleware_changes: Updated event handlers to trigger save on checkbox check and soft delete on uncheck, replacing the batch save button workflow.
implementation_notes: The save-on-check behavior had existed in an earlier version but was reverted. It was re-enabled along with the existing soft-delete-on-uncheck behavior. Initial testing missed the change because it hadn't been pushed to staging.
comment_summary: Developer noted that save-on-check had been implemented previously and was re-enabled. Initial review found the change wasn't pushed to staging, which was quickly corrected. Feature was approved after deployment.
